item.text: The dispatcher-owned summary is already marked `completed`, `handoff_requested` is false, and the repo only shows report-file modifications now. I’m doing one last content sanity check on the key scoped files, then I’ll close out with the final run summary.

task_uid: `019d9996-f8a9-736b-93d9-de5a3e5e447f`  
task_id: `W03-003`  
status: `completed`

What changed: auth login now depends on a minimal repository-facing lookup contract instead of touching legacy model types directly, with the persistence mapping contained in `internal/repository/users`. The scoped analytics/auth-adjacent seams and affected test doubles were updated to use boundary-facing contracts, and application wiring now passes the users repository edge into login handling. Remaining `socialpredict/models` usage in this wave is constrained to explicit repository edges and tests.
